column13: Control is channel & appears clear. Some shore ice, not affecting flow.
column14: Wading (FT2) measurement, ~6m u/s gauge. Wading section fair, irregular bottom but ok. High confidence measurement.   @ Uncertainty: IVE method, 2-sigma value (2 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.ft File). @ DWL at sensor line w/stilling bucket. Orifice looks ok, intacked, full tank (1900psi), feed is 38psi. Indicating no gas usage. Bead is on bottom of arctic purge, further indicating no gas flow. Course and fine valves were open upon arrival however no reaction when adjusted. Purge sounded good, bubbles detected. Bead did not move after purge. Checked for leaks with snoop, none apparent. Good orifice placement, secured with rocks in deepest part of channel. Note that at this stage there is a standing wave directly above orifice. Reset done twice upon arrival as adjustments were made during first reset. Wade measured rough depth of orifice 0.34m - matches well with offset/reading (see below). Unable to see system bubbles in river as too turbulent.   No HWM (no peak stage observed on hydrograph since previously obtained HWM). Depth of HG sensor at 10:25: DWL(5.342) - Offset(5.05) = 0.292m.  Was finally able to get response from BOTH AP beads after adjusting course and fine . Still recommending replacing Arctic purge system or installing Bubbler system at this station. Final reset done after few readings to ensure stablization after bead response.   Multiple resets as we attempted to improve conditions for orifice (removed rock to elimated standing wave) in vacinity. Not concerned with reset 35mins after final DWL as DWL value did not change significantly pre/post mmnt. No good visible gauge pool to reposition orifice to at this stage.   HG1 - sutron accubar -FEEDING STAGE WORKING -HG2- FTS Radar - better than HG1 when enough water. Did not reset radar as hitting ice.Will come back on line when water rises.Surveyed ice below radar sensor (5.875m) logger 5.918m.   RFT: please remove unusable grade if station appears to be working after maintenance work done today. Thank you. Mid-section

column13: Mostly 1 channel in front of gauge and d/s of gauge at this level, 2 channels u/s with most flow coming from River Left channel.
column14: Measurement appears too large in relation to other 2022 measurements.  I have personally seen poor data quality with the use of the ARC boat and a tether system (i.e. nose of boat riding up in turbulent water and sensor therefore collecting uncertain data). Unknown if this occurred here. Many duplicate panels were acquired during the measurement. It appears greater velocity panels were selected to compute the original discharge of 23.1cms. Selecting the lower velocity panels computes a discharge of 19.9cms.  Shift analysis: The 08-01 measurement suggests significant scour in relation to the other 2022 measurements. While not impossible at this station, it would invalidate theory of stability above ~6m gauge height and therefore necessitate a larger ESTIMATED grade.  Reducing the discharge to 19.9cms allows use of the knee-bend shifting regime. Early 2023 measurements will hopefully validate this theory.   SxS Streampro ADCP mmnt made with flying fox and ARC boat. RSxS measurement notes: Panel distances made with Range Finder and level rod. Turbulence and .45m standing waves in mmnt section, Tried using River Ray but appeared to be under measuring. Re-tried Streampro set deeper and worked ok.  Pulled up orifice line and added 2 more metal plates to make it heavier, now ~60lbs. reset, orifice now in ~0.54m of water at this level, new offset 5.102m Mid-section

Discharge Remarks: Used HG2 for MGH because it was more accurate based on DWL. DWL done after Q mmnt but that was lost due to eHSN crashing. SxS measurement notes: Fast, very turbulent water,occasional large debris, lots of small roots and coarse silt in water.  Could hear rocks knocking on river bottom in main channel close to gauge/rock outcrop. Used Cable Fox and High Speed Tethered boat for mmnt. ADCP unable to measure any velocity and struggled to measure depth from 6-14m on mmnt section. Not sure if there was too much turbulence, debris, or rolling rocks, or tethered boat being lifted out of water and getting air entrainment. Was able to get a depth at 8m from ADCP mmnt, original depth was 2.08m, but after review of panel in WinRiver it looked like there were errors caused by the vertical beam depths. After depths changed to BT they looked more consistent for panels at 8m and 11m. Still couldn't get good velocity data for this area of the river so surface vel measured by throwing sticks in and timing travel over a measured distance. Distance was 20m and times were 4.5, 4.5, 4.44, 4.97, 4.9, & 4.2. Average time was 4.585s, average surface vel was 4.362, average velocity (surface vel x 0.88) was 3.839m/s. This was applied to the panel at 11m, and an adjusted velocity was applied at 8m that averaged the measured velocity at 5.5m and the estimated velocity at 11m.  The panel at 22m had some errors with velocity mmmnts that biased the measured vel low, used the measured depth and applied a velocity based on measured adjacent panels. Only one cell from 23m to 30m, but depths look ok and velocities seem reasonable, also only ~5% of river's flow in this area.  Over all not the best mmnt, but probably the best mmnt that could be done within limits of equipment. Mmnt probably +/- 5 to 10% in accuracy I would guess.  5.00 metres below gauge MGH has had sensor reset applied at upload (6.535 + 0.007) Sources:eHSN v1.3.2

Removed panels from 2.5 and 10m because of problems with velocity profiles, looks like velocity was under measured in these panels. Had SxS bug on mmnt, but seems to be fixed by program.
